subject: Ecology
title: Wetlands as Water Filters
description: A 60-second lesson on Wetlands as Water Filters.

hook.text: Wetlands clean water for free—doing what treatment plants do with expensive technology.

buildup.text: Plants absorb excess nitrogen and phosphorus. Microbes break down pollutants in the sediment.

discovery.text: Sediment settles in slow-moving wetland water, trapping heavy metals and suspended particles.

twist.text: New York City saved $6 billion by protecting wetlands instead of building a new filtration plant.

climax.text: Constructed wetlands now treat sewage, agricultural runoff, and even mine drainage worldwide.

punchline.text: The cheapest water filter grows in mud.

quiz.question: How do wetlands remove pollutants from water?
options: Plants absorb nutrients and microbes break down toxins
They boil water with geothermal heat
